original_reasoning: حطم is crushing or breaking; scrapings or shavings are the direct material result of such breaking.
Arabic زفر carries strong exhalation or forceful breath in its root family, and Greek Zephyros is the wind; this is a clear breath-to-wind chain.
Arabic denotes a fabric kind, and Greek 'made of palm fibre' preserves the material-textile domain clearly.
A rolled or twisted form directly supports the idea of a small rounded lump or mass.
Arabic trimming/cutting and Greek carving/engraving align clearly in the domain of cutting-shaping a surface.
Arabic دال (sneaking gait; to conceal/trick) connects to Greek Δέλτα via Phoenician Dāleth (door/guide). Clear graphemic-phonemic cognate pair Semitic D → Greek D (Δ).
Arabic دلل (guide, evidence, to show the way) connects to Greek Δέλτα via Phoenician Dāleth (door/guide). Clear graphemic-phonemic cognate pair Semitic D → Greek D (Δ).
سلاح السباع is an instrument of splitting or tearing, close to dividing in two.
